steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea. The passage opens broadly (animals use tools), then pivots with 'though' and 'busted the myth': the old belief that only primates use tools is proven wrong. Sea otters and woodpecker finches are examples supporting that reversal.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Animals find it difficult to use objects as tools.: The passage says animals use tools to achieve goals 'more easily' — the opposite of difficult. ✗
  • Choice B — Unlike primates, sea otters are exceptionally skillful at using different tools.: The passage never ranks sea otters above primates in skill; it just says both can use tools. ✗
  • Choice C — Contrary to long-held beliefs, primates aren't the only animals to use tools.: This directly matches 'people thought tool use was unique to primates' being overturned. ✓
  • Choice D — There are many myths about tool use that researchers are starting to challenge.: The passage busts one specific myth, not many; 'starting to challenge' also understates what the text presents as already settled. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
